deliverables: Parti diagram and design narrative emphasizing horizon-axis organization as core design driver
Preliminary floor plan sketches (1:200 scale minimum) — 10 programmed spaces across 356 sqm total
Site placement / orientation concept — Pacific-facing horizon axis, wind shelter strategy, scrub-to-sand transition integration
3D conceptual massing model
Material and color direction board — coastal palette: salt-air resistant materials, wind-rated glazing, native scrub integration
Space arrangement diagram showing all 10 programmed spaces
Floor plans all levels (1:100 scale)
Elevations all four facades (1:100) — coastal wind exposure documented
Building sections minimum 2 (1:100)
Roof plan with wind load design notes
Site plan with property boundaries, setbacks, access, coastal zone setback line
Interior design criteria document (coordination with TBD interior designer)
Window and door schedule — wind-rated specifications for coastal exposure
Preliminary structural coordination sketch — wind load study inputs integrated
Pool design coordination (pool structure in scope — engineering in Phase 3)
Solar system preliminary layout (in scope per client brief — system sizing in Phase 3)
Landscape coordination criteria document (landscape architect TBD — coordination in scope)
Structural engineering drawings — wind load design per estudio de cargas de viento (required site document)
Electrical single-line diagram and panel schedules
Lighting layout plan with fixture specifications — coastal IP-rated fixtures specified
Plumbing and hydraulic layout including pool plumbing
Solar system layout and sizing (in scope per special_features)
Foundation plan and details — coastal soil conditions applied
Pool structural engineering coordination
Landscape coordination: referral to landscape architect (TBD at client's choice); integration criteria provided by Oficio Taller
Fully coordinated construction drawing set (architectural + structural + MEP)
Detail sheets: wall sections, roof details, coastal exposure connection details, openings
Finish schedule (all surfaces — salt-air resistant specifications throughout)
Door and window schedule with wind-rated specifications
Built-in furniture drawings (kitchen, closets, built-in shelving)
Technical specifications document
Bid package preparation (executive plans + spec document — minimum 2 contractors)
Bid comparison matrix (contractor name, total, key line items, timeline, coastal construction experience notes)
Recommendation with rationale — local BCS contractors with coastal residential experience prioritized
SEMARNAT coastal zone permit application (Permiso de Zona Federal Marítimo Terrestre) — critical path item; process begins as early as possible
Municipal building permit application (Municipio de Los Cabos)
Submission to both authorities
Tracking and correction management until permits issued
scope_phases.duration_weeks: 16-24 (coastal zone permit SEMARNAT process is critical path)

payment_schedule.name: Concept Design Approved
payment_schedule.trigger_event: Client written approval of Phase 1 conceptual design

payment_schedule.name: Executive Plans Approved
payment_schedule.trigger_event: Executive plans package approved by client

exclusions: Structural, electrical, plumbing, civil, and solar engineering fees (client contracts engineers directly; Oficio Taller coordinates)
Permit fees, SEMARNAT charges, municipal taxes, and government charges (client responsibility)
Contractor selection or construction contracts (Oficio Taller recommends; client decides)
Furniture, art, décor, or movable equipment not shown in built-in furniture drawings
Topographic survey — required site document; client provides
Wind load study — required site document; client provides
Coastal zone permit (SEMARNAT) — Paco prepares application; client signs and is named applicant
Landscape architect and interior designer fees (referral services; clients contracts directly)
Pool contractor selection or construction (Oficio Taller provides engineering coordination and design specs only)
Construction cost guarantees (estimates are preliminary; final cost determined by contractor bids)
Construction supervision (Phase 7 — available as added service; not included in base scope)

revision_assumptions.phase_3: 1 round of structural coordination revision included. Client program additions billed additionally.
revision_assumptions.phase_4: 1 round of coordination revision included. Client changes after executive plan approval require a change order.

project_type_clauses.title: Standard Residential Scope
project_type_clauses.text: This project is a private standalone residential building on an individually owned lot. All design decisions are made exclusively between the client and Oficio Taller unless otherwise noted. No HOA, covenant, or third-party design review applies unless disclosed by the client before contract signing.

project_type_clauses.title: Client-Initiated Program Changes
project_type_clauses.text: Changes to the approved program requested by the client after a phase milestone has been approved will be scoped and billed as a change order. Oficio Taller will not absorb rework costs from client-initiated changes.

project_type_clauses.title: Site Condition Discovery
project_type_clauses.text: Design is based on information provided by the client and publicly available municipal records. If site conditions discovered during construction (soil, utilities, encroachments) require design changes, these will be scoped as a change order.

project_type_clauses.title: Electronic Signature
project_type_clauses.text: This document is valid when signed electronically by the client and an authorized Oficio Taller representative. Electronic signatures carry the same legal weight as physical signatures under Mexican federal law (NOM-151-SCFI).
